claimText: The Universal Immunization Programme (UIP) provides free immunization against 12 diseases in India.
evidenceExcerpt: UIP provides free immunization against 12 diseases including Diphtheria, Polio, Measles, Rubella, Rotavirus, Pneumococcal Pneumonia, and Japanese Encephalitis.
examUse: Statement-based question on the scope and coverage of the UIP.
distractorHooks: Inclusion of non-covered diseases like Malaria, Dengue, or COVID-19

claimText: The Universal Immunization Programme operates as a central component under the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM).
evidenceExcerpt: The programme operates as a central component under the National Rural Health Mission (NRHM).
examUse: Statement-based question on the administrative framework and parent mission of the UIP.

claimText: India has achieved Polio-Free status and eliminated Neonatal tetanus as part of the Universal Immunization Programme's successes.
evidenceExcerpt: Key achievements of the UIP include making India Polio-Free and eliminating Neonatal tetanus.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's official disease elimination achievements.

claimText: The U-WIN Platform was launched to digitalize all vaccination services provided under the Universal Immunization Programme.
evidenceExcerpt: The U-WIN Platform was launched to digitalize all vaccination services provided under the UIP.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching digital health platforms to their specific functions.
